other_observers: After posting my sighting of 2/23 on COBirds, four others came by and successfully viewed it: Bill Maynard, Mark Peterson, Brad Steger, and Saraiya Ruano.; I am the original finder and identifier.
specific_location: Backyard deck of my house, 2220 Capra Way, 80919, in NW CO Spgs.
description: Same size as white-crowned sparrows & juncos feeding with it. Crown distinctly yellow, with two-toned bill--clearly different from the immature white-crowns present with it.Behaviors: Feeding on seed spilled from elevated platform feeder on the edge of my deck. Much more furtive & skittish than the white-crowns; makes only 5-second appearances typically, max of 10 seconds. Skitters off deck into shrubs immediately at any movement inside house (as I took pictures). Only appears on days with light snow, or mornings after nights with light snow. Does not show on warm/sunny days.Call: N.A.Plumage: 1st-winter
photographer: Photographer: Bill Maynard, Marty Wolf, Marty Wolf, Marty Wolf
